applied_optimizations.name: channels_last_3d memory format
applied_optimizations.description: Convert conv weight and input to torch.channels_last_3d memory format for better performance on AMD GPUs

attempted_but_failed.name: Custom Triton kernel
attempted_but_failed.reason: Complexity of 3D transposed convolution with groups=4, stride=(2,2,2), padding=(1,2,3), output_padding=(1,1,1). Multiple attempts with different indexing strategies failed correctness checks or caused GPU memory faults.
attempted_but_failed.error: Memory access fault or output mismatch

attempted_but_failed.name: torch.compile mode=max-autotune
attempted_but_failed.reason: Very slow compilation and execution (2.98ms vs 0.445ms reference)

attempted_but_failed.name: CUDA graph capture
attempted_but_failed.reason: Incompatible with torch.compile's internal graph capture
attempted_but_failed.error: Cannot prepare for replay during capturing stage

attempted_but_failed.name: cudnn.benchmark = True
attempted_but_failed.reason: Affected both models equally, no net benefit

top_bottlenecks: Native ConvTranspose3d is already highly optimized

notes: PyTorch's native ConvTranspose3d with groups=4 is already highly optimized on ROCm
Custom Triton kernel implementation is extremely complex for 3D transposed convolution with all parameters (groups, stride, padding, output_padding)
Final result: 0.489ms vs reference 0.445ms - still ~10% slower
Score: 50.0 (correct but not faster than baseline)
Stage 1 baseline/profile completed - established working baseline but couldn't beat reference
Would require custom tuned kernel or deeper MIOpen integration to beat reference
